summary.intent: Implement task #267: TreeView agnostic core component (+ DnD reorder variant) in a Rust UI component library, following spec-driven TDD workflow with spec sync and audit.
summary.outcome: Full TreeView machine implemented with Key-based identity, expand/collapse, selection (disabled-aware), roving active-descendant focus, keyboard nav, typeahead, DnD agnostic surface, and controlled-prop syncing. 85 unit tests, 26 snapshots, spec-conformance, proptest, 99% line/region coverage; clippy clean; spec fully synced. PR committed and ready.

repo: TreeCollection::nodes() returns ONLY visible nodes (iterates visible_indices), not all nodes — ExpandAll silently breaks without all_nodes()/all_keys() accessors, which had to be added to ars-collections.

Key type does not implement Borrow<str>, so the spec's String-based node identity (.contains(id), BTreeSet<Key>) cannot compile as written; Key::int(5) and Key::from("5") never match, breaking round-trip for integer keys.
selection::State::select already rejects disabled keys internally, but disabled_keys must be explicitly seeded from TreeItem.disabled at init — the guard is a no-op if the set is empty.
EffectName has a blanket impl across all components — no manual impl needed.
Callback<T> implements Clone + Debug + PartialEq (pointer identity) — safe to store in Props alongside an Effect for the same callback event (checkbox pattern).
KeyboardKey has no Character variant — typed characters come from KeyboardEventData.character: Option<char>.
All sibling input and navigation components implement on_props_changed for controlled-prop syncing; omitting it silently breaks controlled use and the DnD re-supply flow.
Navigation integration tests append to a single navigation.rs file (not per-component split).
CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md — must resolve before editing.
.agents/ and .claude/ skill files share the same inode (hardlinked) — editing either updates both.
spec-compile-snippets does syn::parse_file syntax checking only (no type resolution) — type mismatches in spec blocks are caught only at cargo build time, not by the spec gate.

DnD primitives (DropPosition, CollectionDropTarget, CollectionDndEvent, DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection, CollectionDndMessages) live in ars-collections::dnd; shared drag::Handle/drop::Indicator component parts do not exist — only drop_zone utility.

DraggableCollection::selection(&self) is a required method that needs live selection state, which the bare collection doesn't own — the trait impl must bundle collection + selection + DOM hit-testing, making it adapter-layer-only.

code.finding: visible_keys() sits in the impl<T: CollectionItem> block; pure-read accessors with no trait bounds belong in the no-bound impl<T> block (lines 134-175).
select() already rejects keys present in disabled_keys, but the set is empty by default — disabled enforcement requires seeding disabled_keys at component init from TreeItem.disabled flags.
Api requires a manual Debug impl (derive fails due to non-Debug fields); accordion's pattern is the canonical model for Part derive, snapshot helpers, and the callback+Effect wiring.
All agnostic DnD primitives live here; Tabs already integrates reorder and is the prior-art reference for the component-side integration pattern.

workflow: TDD red-first is enforced: write failing tests, confirm compile failure, implement, confirm green — skipping the red step would have missed the disabled_keys init gap.
Insta stops at the first unaccepted snapshot per test function; use INSTA_UPDATE=always (or cargo insta review --accept) after all tests run to generate the full snapshot set at once.
cargo-mutants buffers progress internally; incremental results land in mutants.out/ — polling the task output mid-run gives incomplete data; wait for completion notification.
Mandatory pre-PR mutation gate removed for agnostic components — surviving mutants are now triaged when Nightly CI surfaces them, not as a local pre-commit requirement.
Killer tests added for mutation gaps are kept regardless of gate status — they improve real behavioral coverage.

cargo lock contention between a foregrounded cargo test run and a backgrounded cargo-mutants run causes the background job to fail with a lock error, not a test failure — re-run once foreground finishes.

friction: TreeCollection::nodes() visibility semantics caused a silent ExpandAll contract violation — not caught until manually verifying the spec against the implementation before writing code.

Spec used String-based node identity throughout but the codebase convention and BTreeSet<Key> made it uncompilable as written — required a design question to the user before planning could be finalized.
The mandatory pre-PR mutation gate ran ~36 minutes per cycle; the second run was stopped mid-flight after the gate was removed, leaving stale fallback wakeups that fired spuriously.
CLAUDE.md being a symlink caused an edit attempt to fail — required shell resolution before editing the real file.
spec-compile-snippets only syntax-checks blocks, so type errors in spec code examples are invisible to the spec gate and only surface during cargo build.
Insta snapshot generation is one-snapshot-per-test-invocation; multi-snapshot test functions require a second accept pass to capture all snapshots.
open_items: Adapter-layer DnD deferred to #366 (TreeView Leptos adapter) and #509 (TreeView Dioxus adapter): DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection trait impls, DOM pointer/keyboard hit-testing, 500ms hover-expand timer, use_drag/use_drop wiring.
Lazy Loading variant (spec §5) and Renamable Nodes variant (spec §6) are out-of-scope gated blocks (rust,no_check) — not implemented in this task.
6 MISSED + 2 timeout mutants from initial mutation run; killer tests were added for all 8 but the mandatory gate was removed — surviving mutants will be triaged by Nightly CI.
Pre-existing doc-link warning on MAX_TREE_DEPTH in tree_collection.rs struct doc (not introduced by this task) — not addressed here.
